primary_topics: baseball bat physics (impedance matching, coefficient of restitution)
magnesium welding and stress corrosion cracking
liquation cracking and hot tearing in aluminum welds
joint efficiency in heat-treatable aluminum alloys
cases_mentioned: scandium baseball bat (Soviet origin, BPF rating)
Naval Surface Weapon Center / China Lake high-speed bat testing
Sindo Kou welding metallurgy textbook (cracking examples)
notes: Lecture covers tail end of aluminum alloy welding metallurgy. Opens with extended digression on scandium baseball bats (Soviet alloy use, bat physics, impedance matching), then short complete unit on magnesium welding (why it's not done — stress corrosion cracking), then aluminum welding metallurgy proper (alloy series, liquation cracking, hot tearing, joint efficiency, freezing range vs. crack susceptibility). Ends with mid-lecture break ('go get breakfast'). Likely continues in lecture 30.
